```typescript
/* ============================================
   Auth Configuration
   Session, cookie, and rate-limit constants.
   ============================================ */

/* ── Session ──────────────────────────────── */

export const SESSION_COOKIE_NAME = "pph-session";

/** JWT signing algorithm. */
export const SESSION_ALG = "HS256";

/** Session lifetime in seconds (7 days). */
export const SESSION_MAX_AGE = 60 * 60 * 24 * 7;

/** Grace period in seconds before a session is considered expired. */
export const SESSION_EXPIRY_THRESHOLD = 60 * 5; // 5 minutes

/* ── Cookie ───────────────────────────────── */

export const cookieOptions = {
  httpOnly: true,
  sameSite: "lax" as const,
  secure: process.env.NODE_ENV === "production",
  path: "/",
  maxAge: SESSION_MAX_AGE,
};

/* ── Rate Limiting ────────────────────────── */

export const RATE_LIMIT = {
  MAX_ATTEMPTS: 5,
  WINDOW_MS: 60 * 1000, // 1 minute
  LOCKOUT_MS: 15 * 60 * 1000, // 15 minutes
} as const;

/* ── Timing Attack Prevention ──────────────── */

/**
 * Pre-computed dummy bcrypt hash used when a user is not found.
 * Ensures bcrypt.compare always runs, preventing timing attacks
 * that could reveal whether an email is registered.
 */
export const DUMMY_HASH =
  "$2a$12$N9qo8uLOickgx2ZMRZoMy.MrMKxXQ9B7vrYqI2ZdD6J9.7KqYqYq2";
```

- Cookie name: `pph-session`
- JWT algorithm: `HS256`
- Session lifetime: **7 days**
- Expiry threshold: 5 minutes
- Rate limit: 5 attempts / 1 minute window, 15-minute lockout
- `DUMMY_HASH` — a pre-computed bcrypt hash used to prevent timing attacks when a user is not found

#### `src/lib/auth/errors.ts`
```typescript
/* ============================================
   Authentication & Authorization Errors
   Custom error classes for granular handling.
   ============================================ */

/** Base error for all auth-related failures. */
export class AuthError extends Error {
  constructor(
    message: string,
    public code: string
  ) {
    super(message);
    this.name = "AuthError";
  }
}

/** Thrown when email/password combination is invalid. */
export class InvalidCredentialsError extends AuthError {
  constructor(message = "البريد الإلكتروني أو كلمة المرور غير صحيحة") {
    super(message, "INVALID_CREDENTIALS");
    this.name = "InvalidCredentialsError";
  }
}

/** Thrown when no session exists or the user is not authenticated. */
export class UnauthorizedError extends AuthError {
  constructor(message = "يجب تسجيل الدخول للوصول إلى هذه الصفحة") {
    super(message, "UNAUTHORIZED");
    this.name = "UnauthorizedError";
  }
}

/** Thrown when the authenticated user lacks the required role. */
export class ForbiddenError extends AuthError {
  constructor(message = "ليس لديك صلاحية للوصول إلى هذه الصفحة") {
    super(message, "FORBIDDEN");
    this.name = "ForbiddenError";
  }
}

/** Thrown when a session token has expired or is otherwise invalid. */
export class ExpiredSessionError extends AuthError {
  constructor(message = "انتهت صلاحية الجلسة، يرجى تسجيل الدخول مرة أخرى") {
    super(message, "EXPIRED_SESSION");
    this.name = "ExpiredSessionError";
  }
}

/** Thrown when too many login attempts have been made. */
export class RateLimitExceededError extends AuthError {
  constructor(message = "تم تجاوز عدد محاولات تسجيل الدخول المسموح بها") {
    super(message, "RATE_LIMIT_EXCEEDED");
    this.name = "RateLimitExceededError";
  }
}
```

**Error hierarchy:** `AuthError` (base) → `InvalidCredentialsError`, `UnauthorizedError`, `ForbiddenError`, `ExpiredSessionError`, `RateLimitExceededError`. All have Arabic default messages and unique error codes.

#### `src/lib/auth/permissions.ts`
```typescript
import { requireRole } from "@/lib/auth/session";
import type { UserRole } from "@/types/auth";

/* ============================================
   Role Permissions & Route Guards
   ============================================ */

/**
 * Role hierarchy for reference:
 *
 * OWNER       — Full access (everything)
 * ADMIN       — Everything except owner settings
 * EDITOR      — Projects + Uploads only
 * MEDIA_BUYER — Read-only dashboard + Clients
 * PHOTOGRAPHER— Uploads only
 */

export { UserRole };

export function requireOwner() { return requireRole("OWNER"); }
export function requireAdmin() { return requireRole("OWNER", "ADMIN"); }
export function requireEditor() { return requireRole("OWNER", "ADMIN", "EDITOR"); }
export function requireMediaBuyer() { return requireRole("OWNER", "ADMIN", "MEDIA_BUYER"); }
export function requirePhotographer() { return requireRole("OWNER", "ADMIN", "EDITOR", "PHOTOGRAPHER"); }

export const ROLE_HIERARCHY: readonly UserRole[] = [
  "OWNER", "ADMIN", "EDITOR", "MEDIA_BUYER", "PHOTOGRAPHER", "CLIENT",
] as const;

export function hasMinimumRole(userRole: UserRole, minRole: UserRole): boolean {
  const userIdx = ROLE_HIERARCHY.indexOf(userRole);
  const minIdx = ROLE_HIERARCHY.indexOf(minRole);
  return userIdx !== -1 && minIdx !== -1 && userIdx <= minIdx;
}
```

Thin wrappers around `requireRole()` with role-specific guard functions. Each "higher" level includes all roles above it in the hierarchy.

```typescript
  "OWNER", "ADMIN", "EDITOR", "MEDIA_BUYER", "PHOTOGRAPHER",
];

export const ADMIN_ROLES: UserRole[] = ["OWNER", "ADMIN"];

export function hasRole(userRole: UserRole | undefined, ...roles: UserRole[]): boolean { ... }
export function isAdmin(userRole: UserRole | undefined): boolean { ... }
export function isOwner(userRole: UserRole | undefined): boolean { ... }
export function canManage(userRole: UserRole | undefined): boolean { ... }

// Server-side guards
export async function requireAuth() { return requireUser(); }
export async function requireAdmin() { return requireRole("OWNER", "ADMIN"); }
export async function requireManagement() { return requireRole(...MANAGEMENT_ROLES); }
export async function getOptionalUser() { return getCurrentUser(); }
```

RBAC utility belt: `hasRole`, `isAdmin`, `isOwner`, `canManage` (pure functions) and `requireAuth`, `requireAdmin`, `requireManagement`, `getOptionalUser` (async server guards). Note: `requireAdmin` here shadows the `permissions.ts` version — they are **identical in behavior but different modules**.

---

### 2. Supabase Clients (`src/lib/supabase/`)

#### `admin.ts` — Service role client
```typescript
import { createClient } from "@supabase/supabase-js";

export function createSupabaseAdminClient() {
  return createClient(
    process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL!,
    process.env.SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY!,
    {
      auth: { autoRefreshToken: false, persistSession: false },
    }
  );
}
```
Uses `SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY` — **bypasses RLS**. Server-only.

#### `client.ts` — Browser client
```typescript
import { createBrowserClient } from "@supabase/ssr";

export function createSupabaseBrowserClient() {
  return createBrowserClient(
    process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL!,
    process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY!
  );
}
```
Uses anon key — safe for client-side.

#### `server.ts` — Server client for SSR
```typescript
import { createServerClient } from "@supabase/ssr";
import { cookies } from "next/headers";

export async function createSupabaseServerClient() {
  const cookieStore = await cookies();
  return createServerClient(
    process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL!,
    process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY!,
    {
      cookies: {
        getAll() { return cookieStore.getAll(); },
        setAll(cookiesToSet) { ... },
      },
    }
  );
}
```
Cookie-aware server client. `setAll` silently ignores errors from Server Components.

---

### 3. Service Layer

#### `src/services/auth.service.ts`
```typescript
export const authService = {
  async login(email: string, password: string, options?: { ip?: string; userAgent?: string }): Promise<AuthenticatedUser> { ... },
  async logout(userId?: string): Promise<void> { ... },
};
```

**`login()` flow:**
1. `checkRateLimit(ip ?? email)` → throws `RateLimitExceededError`
2. `userRepository.findByEmail(email)`
3. **Always** runs `bcrypt.compare(password, user?.password ?? DUMMY_HASH)` — timing-safe
4. If invalid → `recordFailedAttempt()`, log failed attempt, throw `InvalidCredentialsError`
5. If valid → `clearRateLimit()`, `createSession()`, log success, return `AuthenticatedUser`

**`logout()` flow:**
1. `destroySession()`
2. If `userId` provided → log `LOGOUT` activity

#### `src/repositories/user.repository.ts`
```typescript
export const userRepository = {
  findById(id: string) { return prisma.user.findUnique({ where: { id } }); },
  findByEmail(email: string) { return prisma.user.findUnique({ where: { email } }); },
  create(data: UserCreateInput) { return prisma.user.create({ data }); },
  update(id: string, data: UserUpdateInput) { return prisma.user.update({ where: { id }, data }); },
  delete(id: string) { return prisma.user.delete({ where: { id } }); },
  findMany(params: { ... }) { return prisma.user.findMany(params); },
  count(where?: UserWhereInput) { return prisma.user.count({ where }); },
};
```
Standard CRUD repository — the only layer that touches Prisma for User operations.

---

### 4. Server Actions (`src/actions/auth/`)

3 files:

#### `login.ts` — `loginAction`
```typescript
export type LoginState = { success: boolean; error?: string; user?: AuthenticatedUser; };

export async function loginAction(_prev: LoginState, formData: FormData): Promise<LoginState> { ... }
export const LOGIN_REDIRECT = ROUTES.DASHBOARD;
```
- Checks if user is **already authenticated** (`getCurrentUser()`) — if so, returns `{ success: true }` immediately
- Parses form data with `loginSchema`
- Extracts `x-forwarded-for` / `x-real-ip` / `user-agent` from headers
- Delegates to `authService.login()`
- Catches `RateLimitExceededError`, `InvalidCredentialsError`, `AuthError`
- Fallback error: "حدث خطأ غير متوقع"

#### `logout.ts` — `logoutAction`
```typescript
export async function logoutAction(): Promise<{ success: boolean }> {
  const user = await getCurrentUser();
  await authService.logout(user?.id);
  return { success: true };
}
export const LOGOUT_REDIRECT = ROUTES.LOGIN;
```

#### `register.ts` — `registerAction`
```typescript
export type RegisterState = { success: boolean; error?: string; user?: AuthenticatedUser; };

export async function registerAction(_prev: RegisterState, formData: FormData): Promise<RegisterState> { ... }
```
- Parses with `registerSchema`
- Checks for duplicate email
- Creates user with **default role: `PHOTOGRAPHER`**
- Automatically `createSession()` on success
- Labeled as "Stabilization stub — To be fully implemented in a future sprint"

---

### 5. Types & Validation

#### `src/types/auth.ts`
```typescript
import type { UserRole as PrismaUserRole } from "@prisma/client";

export type UserRole = PrismaUserRole;
export type AuthenticatedUser = { id: string; email: string; name: string; role: UserRole; avatar?: string | null; };
export type SessionUser = AuthenticatedUser | null;
export type SessionStatus = "loading" | "authenticated" | "unauthenticated";
```

#### `src/lib/validations/auth.ts`
Zod schemas (all Arabic error messages):
- `emailSchema` — required, valid email
- `passwordSchema` — min 8 chars, 1 uppercase, 1 lowercase, 1 digit
- `nameSchema` — 2–50 chars
- `phoneSchema` — Saudi phone regex (`/^(\+?966|0)?5\d{8}$/`), optional
- `loginSchema` — `{ email, password }` (password only checks non-empty)
- `registerSchema` — `{ name, email, password, confirmPassword }` + refinement for match

---

### 6. Prisma Schema — UserRole Enum
From `prisma/schema.prisma:17`:
```prisma
enum UserRole {
  OWNER
  ADMIN
  EDITOR
  MEDIA_BUYER
  PHOTOGRAPHER
  CLIENT
}
```
The User model field: `role UserRole @default(PHOTOGRAPHER)` at line 245.

Client-side context. `setUser` auto-updates `status` to `"authenticated"` or `"unauthenticated"`. Throws if used outside provider.

- `ROUTES.LOGIN` → `/login`
- `ROUTES.DASHBOARD` → `/dashboard`

Sidebar configuration with `NAV_SECTIONS` (13 items) and `NAV_FOOTER_ITEMS` (Settings, Profile, **Logout** — with `action: "logout"`).

#### `src/components/shared/user-menu.tsx`

#### `src/components/shared/sidebar-item.tsx`

### Architecture Summary

```
┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│               UI Components                       │
│  UserMenu, SidebarItem (via useSession context)   │
└─────────────────┬────────────────────────────────┘
                  │ Server Actions
┌─────────────────▼────────────────────────────────┐
│  loginAction / logoutAction / registerAction      │
│  (src/actions/auth/)                              │
└─────────────────┬────────────────────────────────┘
                  │
┌─────────────────▼────────────────────────────────┐
│          authService (src/services/)              │
│  Rate-limit → userRepository → bcrypt → session  │
└───────┬───────────────────┬──────────────────────┘
        │                   │
┌───────▼──────┐   ┌────────▼──────────┐
│ userRepository│   │ session.ts (jose) │
│ (Prisma)      │   │ create/get/       │
│               │   │ require/destroy   │
└───────────────┘   └────────┬──────────┘
                             │
               ┌─────────────▼───────────┐
               │  permissions.ts / rbac.ts │
               │  requireOwner/Admin/etc.  │
               └───────────────────────────┘
```

**Key observations:**
- **No `middleware.ts`** exists anywhere in the project — there is no route-level auth guarding at the Next.js middleware layer
- Session is purely JWT-based (using `jose`), completely independent of Supabase Auth
- The Supabase client (server/browser/admin) exists but the **custom JWT auth system does not use Supabase Auth** for user identity
- There are **two parallel** RBAC modules (`permissions.ts` and `rbac.ts`) with overlapping functionality
- All error messages are in **Arabic**
- Default role for new registrations is `PHOTOGRAPHER`
- The rate limiter is **in-memory only** — will not work across multiple server instances

This tool executes a given shell command using the best available Windows shell (PowerShell Core, Windows PowerShell, or Command Prompt).

IMPORTANT:
- This tool is for terminal operations (e.g., git / npm / docker / build & test commands).
- DO NOT use this tool for file operations like reading, writing, editing, or searching files. Prefer the dedicated file tools for those actions.
- Quote any file path that contains spaces with double quotes, e.g. `cd "/path with spaces"` or `python "/path with spaces/script.py"`.
- If a command would produce very large output, prefer adding flags/filters (narrow paths, limit counts, request specific fields, machine-readable formats like JSON) to keep output small and unambiguous.
- Very large outputs may be returned as the most recent tail of the output. Earlier content may be truncated from the beginning. If the tool reports a "Full output saved to: <path>", that file contains the full command output. Use the exact path returned by the tool, then prefer searching that file first (grep/rg or platform equivalents) for specific ERROR/WARN/FAIL/SUCCESS markers, then reading small slices (with `read_file` using `offset`/`limit`) or tailing the log file instead of re-running expensive commands.
- Do not append truncating pipelines such as `| tail`, `| Select-Object -Last`, or similar to an expensive first run just to suppress output. Those discard earlier context and force a re-run if you later need omitted lines. Let the tool/log capture the full output, then inspect the saved log file.
- When issuing multiple shell commands in one response, parallelize only when they are truly independent and do not compete for the same files, directories, logs, locks, caches, git state, or ports. If one command depends on another or shared state may race, run them sequentially.

The following information is returned:

- Directory: Directory where command was executed, or `(root)`.
- Output: Combined command output (stdout/stderr). Can be `(empty)` or an excerpt if very large.
- Error: Error or `(none)` if no error was reported for the subprocess.
- Exit Code: Exit code or `(none)` if terminated by signal.

### 1. Auth Core Library (`src/lib/auth/`)

**8 files** found:

#### `src/lib/auth/config.ts`
```typescript
/* ============================================
   Auth Configuration
   Session, cookie, and rate-limit constants.
   ============================================ */

/* ── Session ──────────────────────────────── */

export const SESSION_COOKIE_NAME = "pph-session";

/** JWT signing algorithm. */
export const SESSION_ALG = "HS256";

/** Session lifetime in seconds (7 days). */
export const SESSION_MAX_AGE = 60 * 60 * 24 * 7;

/** Grace period in seconds before a session is considered expired. */
export const SESSION_EXPIRY_THRESHOLD = 60 * 5; // 5 minutes

/* ── Cookie ───────────────────────────────── */

export const cookieOptions = {
  httpOnly: true,
  sameSite: "lax" as const,
  secure: process.env.NODE_ENV === "production",
  path: "/",
  maxAge: SESSION_MAX_AGE,
};

/* ── Rate Limiting ────────────────────────── */

export const RATE_LIMIT = {
  MAX_ATTEMPTS: 5,
  WINDOW_MS: 60 * 1000, // 1 minute
  LOCKOUT_MS: 15 * 60 * 1000, // 15 minutes
} as const;

/* ── Timing Attack Prevention ──────────────── */

/**
 * Pre-computed dummy bcrypt hash used when a user is not found.
 * Ensures bcrypt.compare always runs, preventing timing attacks
 * that could reveal whether an email is registered.
 */
export const DUMMY_HASH =
  "$2a$12$N9qo8uLOickgx2ZMRZoMy.MrMKxXQ9B7vrYqI2ZdD6J9.7KqYqYq2";
```

**Key constants:**
- Cookie name: `pph-session`
- JWT algorithm: `HS256`
- Session lifetime: **7 days**
- Expiry threshold: 5 minutes
- Rate limit: 5 attempts / 1 minute window, 15-minute lockout
- `DUMMY_HASH` — a pre-computed bcrypt hash used to prevent timing attacks when a user is not found

---

#### `src/lib/auth/errors.ts`
```typescript
/* ============================================
   Authentication & Authorization Errors
   Custom error classes for granular handling.
   ============================================ */

/** Base error for all auth-related failures. */
export class AuthError extends Error {
  constructor(
    message: string,
    public code: string
  ) {
    super(message);
    this.name = "AuthError";
  }
}

/** Thrown when email/password combination is invalid. */
export class InvalidCredentialsError extends AuthError {
  constructor(message = "البريد الإلكتروني أو كلمة المرور غير صحيحة") {
    super(message, "INVALID_CREDENTIALS");
    this.name = "InvalidCredentialsError";
  }
}

/** Thrown when no session exists or the user is not authenticated. */
export class UnauthorizedError extends AuthError {
  constructor(message = "يجب تسجيل الدخول للوصول إلى هذه الصفحة") {
    super(message, "UNAUTHORIZED");
    this.name = "UnauthorizedError";
  }
}

/** Thrown when the authenticated user lacks the required role. */
export class ForbiddenError extends AuthError {
  constructor(message = "ليس لديك صلاحية للوصول إلى هذه الصفحة") {
    super(message, "FORBIDDEN");
    this.name = "ForbiddenError";
  }
}

/** Thrown when a session token has expired or is otherwise invalid. */
export class ExpiredSessionError extends AuthError {
  constructor(message = "انتهت صلاحية الجلسة، يرجى تسجيل الدخول مرة أخرى") {
    super(message, "EXPIRED_SESSION");
    this.name = "ExpiredSessionError";
  }
}

/** Thrown when too many login attempts have been made. */
export class RateLimitExceededError extends AuthError {
  constructor(message = "تم تجاوز عدد محاولات تسجيل الدخول المسموح بها") {
    super(message, "RATE_LIMIT_EXCEEDED");
    this.name = "RateLimitExceededError";
  }
}
```

**Error hierarchy:** `AuthError` (base) → `InvalidCredentialsError`, `UnauthorizedError`, `ForbiddenError`, `ExpiredSessionError`, `RateLimitExceededError`. All have Arabic default messages and unique error codes.

---

#### `src/lib/auth/session.ts`

```typescript
import { cookies } from "next/headers";
import { SignJWT, jwtVerify } from "jose";

import {
  SESSION_COOKIE_NAME,
  SESSION_ALG,

} from "@/lib/auth/config";
import {
  UnauthorizedError,

import type { SessionUser, UserRole } from "@/types/auth";

/* ============================================

   JWT-based session management using jose.
   Works in both Node.js and Edge runtimes.
   ============================================ */

function getSecret(): Uint8Array {
  const secret = process.env.AUTH_SECRET;
  if (!secret) {
    throw new Error("AUTH_SECRET environment variable is required");
  }
  return new TextEncoder().encode(secret);
}

/** JWT payload shape (internal). */
type SessionPayload = {

  email: string;
  name: string;
  role: UserRole;
  avatar?: string | null;

export async function createSession(user: {
  id: string; email: string; name: string; role: UserRole; avatar?: string | null;
}): Promise<void> { ... }

export async function getSession(): Promise<SessionUser> { ... }

export async function getCurrentUser(): Promise<SessionUser> { ... }

export async function requireUser(): Promise<NonNullable<SessionUser>> { ... }

export async function requireRole(
  ...allowedRoles: UserRole[]

export async function destroySession(): Promise<void> { ... }
```

| Function | Returns | Throws |
|---|---|---|
| `createSession(user)` | `void` | — |
| `getSession()` | `SessionUser` (nullable) | — (returns null) |
| `getCurrentUser()` | `SessionUser` (nullable) | — (returns null) |
| `requireUser()` | `NonNullable<SessionUser>` | `UnauthorizedError`, `ExpiredSessionError` |

| `destroySession()` | `void` | — |

Uses `jose` (not `jsonwebtoken`) for Edge compatibility. Secret comes from `AUTH_SECRET` env var.

#### `src/lib/auth/rate-limit.ts`
```typescript

import { RateLimitExceededError } from "@/lib/auth/errors";

/* ============================================
   Rate Limiter
   In-memory rate limiting for login attempts.
   Tracks attempts per IP address within a sliding
   window. Suitable for single-instance deployments.
   ============================================ */

type AttemptRecord = {
  count: number;
  windowStart: number;
  lockedUntil: number;
};

const attempts = new Map<string, AttemptRecord>();

export function checkRateLimit(key: string): void { ... }

export function _resetRateLimit(): void { ... }
```

**In-memory** sliding window rate limiter. Single-instance only (not clustered). Uses `Map<string, AttemptRecord>`. Has a `pruneStale()` cleanup that runs on each check. Exports `_resetRateLimit()` for test teardown.

---

#### `src/lib/auth/permissions.ts`
```typescript
import { requireRole } from "@/lib/auth/session";
import type { UserRole } from "@/types/auth";

/* ============================================
   Role Permissions & Route Guards
   ============================================ */

/**
 * Role hierarchy for reference:
 *
 * OWNER       — Full access (everything)
 * ADMIN       — Everything except owner settings
 * EDITOR      — Projects + Uploads only
 * MEDIA_BUYER — Read-only dashboard + Clients
 * PHOTOGRAPHER— Uploads only
 */

export { UserRole };

export function requireOwner() { return requireRole("OWNER"); }
export function requireAdmin() { return requireRole("OWNER", "ADMIN"); }
export function requireEditor() { return requireRole("OWNER", "ADMIN", "EDITOR"); }
export function requireMediaBuyer() { return requireRole("OWNER", "ADMIN", "MEDIA_BUYER"); }
export function requirePhotographer() { return requireRole("OWNER", "ADMIN", "EDITOR", "PHOTOGRAPHER"); }

export const ROLE_HIERARCHY: readonly UserRole[] = [
  "OWNER", "ADMIN", "EDITOR", "MEDIA_BUYER", "PHOTOGRAPHER", "CLIENT",
] as const;

export function hasMinimumRole(userRole: UserRole, minRole: UserRole): boolean {
  const userIdx = ROLE_HIERARCHY.indexOf(userRole);
  const minIdx = ROLE_HIERARCHY.indexOf(minRole);
  return userIdx !== -1 && minIdx !== -1 && userIdx <= minIdx;
}
```

Thin wrappers around `requireRole()` with role-specific guard functions. Each "higher" level includes all roles above it in the hierarchy.

---

#### `src/lib/auth/rbac.ts`
```typescript
import { getCurrentUser, requireUser, requireRole } from "@/lib/auth/session";
import type { UserRole } from "@prisma/client";

export const ALL_ROLES: UserRole[] = [
  "OWNER", "ADMIN", "EDITOR", "MEDIA_BUYER", "PHOTOGRAPHER", "CLIENT",
];

export const MANAGEMENT_ROLES: UserRole[] = [
  "OWNER", "ADMIN", "EDITOR", "MEDIA_BUYER", "PHOTOGRAPHER",
];

export const ADMIN_ROLES: UserRole[] = ["OWNER", "ADMIN"];

export function hasRole(userRole: UserRole | undefined, ...roles: UserRole[]): boolean { ... }
export function isAdmin(userRole: UserRole | undefined): boolean { ... }
export function isOwner(userRole: UserRole | undefined): boolean { ... }
export function canManage(userRole: UserRole | undefined): boolean { ... }

// Server-side guards
export async function requireAuth() { return requireUser(); }
export async function requireAdmin() { return requireRole("OWNER", "ADMIN"); }
export async function requireManagement() { return requireRole(...MANAGEMENT_ROLES); }
export async function getOptionalUser() { return getCurrentUser(); }
```

RBAC utility belt: `hasRole`, `isAdmin`, `isOwner`, `canManage` (pure functions) and `requireAuth`, `requireAdmin`, `requireManagement`, `getOptionalUser` (async server guards). ### 3. Service Layer

#### `src/services/auth.service.ts`
```typescript
export const authService = {
  async login(email: string, password: string, options?: { ip?: string; userAgent?: string }): Promise<AuthenticatedUser> { ... },
  async logout(userId?: string): Promise<void> { ... },
};
```

**`login()` flow:**
1. `checkRateLimit(ip ?? email)` → throws `RateLimitExceededError`
2. `userRepository.findByEmail(email)`
3. **Always** runs `bcrypt.compare(password, user?.password ?? DUMMY_HASH)` — timing-safe
4. If invalid → `recordFailedAttempt()`, log failed attempt, throw `InvalidCredentialsError`
5. If valid → `clearRateLimit()`, `createSession()`, log success, return `AuthenticatedUser`

**`logout()` flow:**
1. `destroySession()`
2. If `userId` provided → log `LOGOUT` activity

#### `src/repositories/user.repository.ts`
```typescript
export const userRepository = {

  findByEmail(email: string) { return prisma.user.findUnique({ where: { email } }); },
  create(data: UserCreateInput) { return prisma.user.create({ data }); },
  update(id: string, data: UserUpdateInput) { return prisma.user.update({ where: { id }, data }); },
  delete(id: string) { return prisma.user.delete({ where: { id } }); },
  findMany(params: { ... }) { return prisma.user.findMany(params); },
  count(where?: UserWhereInput) { return prisma.user.count({ where }); },
};
```
Standard CRUD repository — the only layer that touches Prisma for User operations.

### Architecture Summary

```
┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────┐

└─────────────────┬────────────────────────────────┘

│               │   │ require/destroy   │
└───────────────┘   └────────┬──────────┘
                             │
               ┌─────────────▼───────────┐
               │  permissions.ts / rbac.ts │
               │  requireOwner/Admin/etc.  │
               └───────────────────────────┘
```

**Key observations:**
- **No `middleware.ts`** exists anywhere in the project — there is no route-level auth guarding at the Next.js middleware layer
- Session is purely JWT-based (using `jose`), completely independent of Supabase Auth
- The Supabase client (server/browser/admin) exists but the **custom JWT auth system does not use Supabase Auth** for user identity
- There are **two parallel** RBAC modules (`permissions.ts` and `rbac.ts`) with overlapping functionality
- All error messages are in **Arabic**
- Default role for new registrations is `PHOTOGRAPHER`
- The rate limiter is **in-memory only** — will not work across multiple server instances

pattern.description: The glob pattern to match against (e.g., '**/*.py', 'docs/*.md').

path.description: Optional: The absolute path to the directory to search within. If omitted, searches the root directory.

maxResults.description: Optional: Maximum number of file paths to return (default: 100). Use to limit output for broad patterns. When the cap is hit the result is paginated; pass 'offset' set to the previous offset+maxResults to continue.

offset.description: Optional: Number of matching file paths to skip before returning results. Use this to continue after a truncated result's next offset.

functionDeclarations.name: read_file
functionDeclarations.description: Reads and returns the content of a specified file. By default reads up to 2000 lines starting from the beginning of the file. Files whose selected range exceeds 256 KB are truncated with an advisory; use 'offset' and 'limit' only if the file is too large to read at once — it's recommended to read the whole file by not providing these parameters. Handles text files. For images, PDFs, videos, audio or other multimedia files, this tool cannot present their contents to the model — use the 'analyze_multimedia' tool instead.

absolute_path.description: The absolute path to the file to read (e.g., '/home/user/project/file.txt'). Relative paths are not supported. You must provide an absolute path.

offset.description: Optional: For text files, the 0-based line number to start reading from. Only provide if the file is too large to read at once. Note: search_file_content reports matches as 1-based `Lxx` line numbers; to jump from a hit at `L42` here, subtract 1 — pass `offset: 41` (i.e. `offset = lineNumber - 1`).

limit.description: Optional: For text files, the maximum number of lines to read. Only provide if the file is too large to read at once.

functionDeclarations.name: run_shell_command
functionDeclarations.description:
This tool executes a given shell command using the best available Windows shell (PowerShell Core, Windows PowerShell, or Command Prompt).

IMPORTANT:
- This tool is for terminal operations (e.g., git / npm / docker / build & test commands).
- DO NOT use this tool for file operations like reading, writing, editing, or searching files. Prefer the dedicated file tools for those actions.
- Quote any file path that contains spaces with double quotes, e.g. `cd "/path with spaces"` or `python "/path with spaces/script.py"`.
- If a command would produce very large output, prefer adding flags/filters (narrow paths, limit counts, request specific fields, machine-readable formats like JSON) to keep output small and unambiguous.
- Very large outputs may be returned as the most recent tail of the output. Earlier content may be truncated from the beginning. If the tool reports a "Full output saved to: <path>", that file contains the full command output. Use the exact path returned by the tool, then prefer searching that file first (grep/rg or platform equivalents) for specific ERROR/WARN/FAIL/SUCCESS markers, then reading small slices (with `read_file` using `offset`/`limit`) or tailing the log file instead of re-running expensive commands.
- Do not append truncating pipelines such as `| tail`, `| Select-Object -Last`, or similar to an expensive first run just to suppress output. Those discard earlier context and force a re-run if you later need omitted lines. Let the tool/log capture the full output, then inspect the saved log file.
- When issuing multiple shell commands in one response, parallelize only when they are truly independent and do not compete for the same files, directories, logs, locks, caches, git state, or ports. If one command depends on another or shared state may race, run them sequentially.

The following information is returned:
- Command: Executed command.
- Directory: Directory where command was executed, or `(root)`.
- Output: Combined command output (stdout/stderr). Can be `(empty)` or an excerpt if very large.
- Error: Error or `(none)` if no error was reported for the subprocess.
- Exit Code: Exit code or `(none)` if terminated by signal.
- Signal: Signal number or `(none)` if no signal was received.

command.description: Exact command to execute using the best available Windows shell (automatically selected from PowerShell Core, Windows PowerShell, or Command Prompt). Quote any file paths containing spaces with double quotes.

description.description: Brief description of the command for the user. Be specific and concise. Ideally a single sentence. Can be up to 3 sentences for clarity. No line breaks.

functionDeclarations.name: search_file_content
functionDeclarations.description: Searches for a regular expression pattern within the content of files in a specified directory, a specific file, or the current working directory. The search backend is ripgrep, so regex patterns use ripgrep/Rust regex syntax, not JavaScript RegExp syntax. If you need to search literal text that contains regex metacharacters (for example $, {, [, (, or \), set fixed_strings: true or -F: true instead of escaping by hand. Can filter files by a glob pattern when searching directories. By default, returns matching lines with file paths and line numbers; set output_mode to "files_with_matches" for just file paths or "count" for per-file match totals. Each matched line is truncated at 500 characters with a "[line truncated from N chars]" suffix to keep minified or base64 content from blowing up context.

pattern.description: The regular expression pattern to search for within file contents. This is interpreted by ripgrep/Rust regex, not JavaScript RegExp; avoid JS-only features such as lookbehind and backreferences. Regex syntax errors are returned by ripgrep. For literal text, especially strings containing regex metacharacters like $, {, [, (, or \, prefer fixed_strings: true or -F: true. When passing JSON args, escape only for JSON once: to match literal ${, use pattern "\\$\\{" (the regex engine sees \$\{), not "\\\\$\\\\{".

path.description: Optional: A path relative to the workspace root, or an absolute path to a directory or file to search within. If a file path is provided, searches only within that file. If a directory path is provided, searches all files in that directory. If omitted, searches the current working directory.

glob.description: Optional: A glob pattern to filter which files are searched (e.g., '*.js', '*.{ts,tsx}', 'src/**'). Single string only — combine multiple patterns via whitespace/comma separation (e.g. '*.js *.ts', '*.js,*.ts') or brace expansion ('*.{ts,tsx}'). Only applicable when searching directories, ignored for file searches. If omitted, searches all files (respecting potential global ignores).

output_mode.description: Optional: Output format. 'content' (default) returns matching lines with line numbers and optional context; 'files_with_matches' returns only the list of matching file paths; 'count' returns per-file match counts.

maxResults.description: Optional: Maximum number of matches to return to prevent context overflow (default: 250, max: 1000). Values above 1000 are clamped automatically. Use lower values for broad searches, higher for specific searches. Pass 0 for unlimited.

head_limit.description: Optional: Claude Code aligned alias for 'maxResults'. When both are provided, head_limit wins. Pass 0 for unlimited (use sparingly — large result sets waste context).

offset.description: Optional: Number of matches to skip before returning results. Use this to continue after a truncated result's next offset.

-A.description: Optional: Number of context lines to show after each match. Only applies to 'content' output_mode. Alias for ripgrep's -A.

-B.description: Optional: Number of context lines to show before each match. Only applies to 'content' output_mode. Alias for ripgrep's -B.

-C.description: Optional: Number of context lines to show before and after each match. Only applies to 'content' output_mode. Alias for ripgrep's -C.

context.description: Optional: Number of context lines to show before and after each match. Only applies to 'content' output_mode. Alias for -C.

-n.description: Optional: Whether to include line numbers in 'content' output. Defaults to true; set to false to omit them.

-i.description: Optional: Case-insensitive matching. Defaults to false (case-sensitive), matching ripgrep behavior.

fixed_strings.description: Optional: Treat the pattern as a literal string instead of a regular expression. Alias for -F.

-F.description: Optional: Treat the pattern as a literal string instead of a regular expression. Alias for fixed_strings.

type.description: Optional: ripgrep file type filter (rg --type). Common types: js, py, rust, go, java, etc. Accepts any alias from `rg --type-list`; the value is forwarded verbatim to ripgrep.

multiline.description: Optional: Enable multiline matching where patterns may span newlines (passes -U and --multiline-dotall to ripgrep). Only meaningful for 'content' output_mode. ^/$ anchors keep line boundaries behavior for portability across search backends.

include_hidden.description: Optional: Whether to descend into dot-prefixed directories (e.g. '.git/', '.codely-cli/') and match dot-prefixed files. Defaults to false to mirror ripgrep's default hidden-skip behaviour. Set to true to scan hidden paths explicitly (passes --hidden to ripgrep).
